forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KXJWNX69AB8Q6PEBEPH5KP3A
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9080'
+title: 'Docs: README, DocC, departures'
+---
+## What
+Per plan.md §10 task 12. Write `README.md` (library-style: declare → fuse → session → CLI, with a runnable example; the diagnostics loop — edit → see compiler errors → fix — front and center), DocC comments on all public API in `Sources/FileTool/`, plan §8 departures cross-referenced in a DESIGN_NOTES section or file, and a note pointing at the upstream `DiagnosticsReport` visibility change.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] README's usage snippet compiles (doc-snippet-tested against the example source, the siblings' mechanism)
+- [ ] Every public type/method in `Sources/FileTool/` has a DocC comment
+- [ ] `swift build` emits no missing-documentation warnings if the docc lint flag is enabled
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/ReadmeSnippetTests.swift`: extract the README code fence(s) and compile/assert against the example source (sibling mechanism)
+- [ ] Run `swift test --filter ReadmeSnippetTests` — expect: green
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

 title: 'Docs: README, DocC, departures'
 ---
 ## What
-Per plan.md §10 task 12. Write `README.md` (library-style: declare → fuse → session → CLI, with a runnable example; the diagnostics loop — edit → see compiler errors → fix — front and center), DocC comments on all public API in `Sources/FileTool/`, plan §8 departures cross-referenced in a DESIGN_NOTES section or file, and a note pointing at the upstream `DiagnosticsReport` visibility change.
+Per plan.md §10 task 12. Write `README.md` (library-style: declare → fuse → session → CLI, with a runnable example; the diagnostics loop — edit → see compiler errors → fix — front and center, including the multi-project story: a session root above several git repos, per-project diagnostics routing), DocC comments on all public API in `Sources/FileTool/`, plan §8 departures cross-referenced in a DESIGN_NOTES section or file, and a note pointing at the upstream `CodeContextManager` + public `CodeContext.rootDirectory` changes this package builds on. DESIGN_NOTES also records the nested-repo semantics (nearest-open-ancestor-wins; overlap degradation to `pending`) per the bridge task.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] README's usage snippet compiles (doc-snippet-tested against the example source, the siblings' mechanism)
 - [ ] Every public type/method in `Sources/FileTool/` has a DocC comment (checked by the snippet test target compiling with documentation warnings surfaced)
-- [ ] DESIGN_NOTES departures section exists and cross-references plan §8 items 1–8
+- [ ] DESIGN_NOTES departures section exists, cross-references plan §8 items 1–8, and documents the nested-repo/overlap semantics

 ## Tests
 - [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/ReadmeSnippetTests.swift`: extract the README code fence(s) and compile/assert against the example source (sibling mechanism)

+  text: 'Research done. Audited public surface of Sources/FileTool: awk scan of `public`-keyword decls (types/methods/props/inits) across all 18 files returns ZERO missing `///`; public enum cases (EditEngine.Resolution/BatchOutcome, DiagnosticsBridge.Mode, ScriptModeError) manually verified documented. Earlier tasks did thorough DocC. Sibling snippet mechanism: FoundationModelsOperationTool/Examples/NotesTool/.../ReadmeSnippetTests.swift uses `<!-- doc-snippet source=\"...\" -->` provenance — asserts each README fence is a contiguous excerpt of a real (compiled) source file. Sibling DocCoverageTests uses SwiftSyntax+TestSupport (deps we lack) — porting its intent as a self-contained line-based `public`-keyword scanner instead. Actual API confirmed: FileContext(root:readOnly:allowSymlinks:eagerWarmup:) (NO diagnostics: label — plan §5 is stale); FileTool.make/makeReadOnly; edit params find/replace/replacesAll/occurrence; CLI grammar `<noun> <verb>` (read uses --path, write/edit --file-path, glob/grep --pattern); --script/--chat flags on FileToolCLI. README fences will cite a new compiled Examples/FileDemo/.../ReadmeExample.swift + main.swift + Sources/FileTool/FileDiagnostics.swift. Starting TDD: writing failing ReadmeSnippetTests first.'
